steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage's conditional claim: “the use of clustered camera trapping and GPS tracking of collars tends to lead to underestimates of cougar population density.” If true, the study using these methods should show the lowest density results.

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
From the table, only Murphy et al. used “clustered camera trapping, GPS tracking of collars” — and reported 1.65, the lowest density among all studies.

This directly proves the underestimate claim: the study with that method produced the lowest results.

The other choices study different methods, skip methodology, or show opposite results.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
